capability_objective: Execute independent searches in parallel, gate each dependent retrieval on that branch's unique stable ID, then compare only retrieved records while respecting read-only and exact-format constraints.
prompt:
The travel desk is reconciling two itineraries before the approval queue closes. Work only through the genuine local executable `./travel-desk`; do not inspect its SQLite database or activity log directly. You may inspect `./travel-desk --help` before starting. Find the trip named "Nairobi Field Training" at "Nairobi" and the trip named "Accra Program Review" at "Accra". Your first travel-desk data action must launch both exact name-and-location searches concurrently in one assistant action, then wait for both results. For each branch, run a get only when that branch's search returned exactly one stable ID. If both branches resolve, launch both gets concurrently together in the next travel-desk data action, after both searches have finished. Never infer an ID. If one search is missing or ambiguous, leave that branch unresolved without guessing and still complete the independently resolved branch.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke the collection list, saved profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify operations. Do not modify `travel-desk`, `.travel-desk/travel.sqlite3`, or `verify_audit.py`. Base the statuses and comparison only on successful get results; search results are not full records.

Write the deliverable to `audit.md` with exactly three Markdown bullets and no heading, blank lines, or other text. Use these literal field labels and separators, substituting retrieved values for angle-bracket placeholders:
- `<first trip name> | ID: <first stable ID> | status: <first status>`
- `<second trip name> | ID: <second stable ID> | status: <second status>`
- `Status comparison | <same or different> | <first stable ID>: <first status> | <second stable ID>: <second status>`
Keep the requested Nairobi record first and Accra record second. When both records resolve, write `same` only if their retrieved status strings are identical; otherwise write `different`. Run `python3 verify_audit.py` when finished.
